title: Elden Ring
note: TGA GOTY 2022; 96 MC; redefined the open-world template entirely; 25M+ copies; SotE DLC is itself a standalone masterwork.

title: Baldur's Gate 3
note: TGA GOTY 2023; 96 MC; genre-defining reactivity and branching; exceptional both solo and in co-op up to four players.

title: Red Dead Redemption 2
note: 97 MC; most cinematic and detail-saturated open world of the generation; player devotion sustained at an extraordinary level seven years on.

title: God of War
note: TGA GOTY 2018; 94 MC; rebooted the franchise and set a generation's prestige-action template for narrative and combat pacing alike.

title: Sekiro: Shadows Die Twice
note: TGA GOTY 2019; 90 MC; peerless posture-based combat; the most skill-pure FromSoft entry and still the best the studio has ever felt to control.

title: Dark Souls III
note: 89 MC; capstone of the trilogy; boss design reaches a series peak before Elden Ring — Nameless King and Soul of Cinder remain benchmarks.

title: Cyberpunk 2077
note: The greatest comeback in gaming; v2.0's reworked systems and Phantom Liberty's spy thriller narrative turned a troubled launch into a top-tier RPG.

title: Resident Evil 2 Remake
note: 93 MC; remake gold standard; made the RE Engine a franchise engine; Mr. X remains the most oppressive level-design device in the genre.

title: Metaphor: ReFantazio
note: 94 MC; the Persona team's fantasy pivot; deep political narrative, flexible job system, and the most fully realized world Atlus has ever built.

title: Metal Gear Solid V: The Phantom Pain
Alan Wake 2
note: 89 MC; multiple TGA 2023 wins; Remedy's most ambitious narrative; live-action sequences are a swing no one else in the medium took.

title: The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt
note: 93 MC; 50M+ copies; richest RPG writing of its era; Dec 2022 PS5 patch adds ray tracing, DualSense haptics, and 60 fps — the definitive version.

title: Persona 5 Royal
note: 95 MC; definitive version of a generation-defining JRPG; the added third semester is the best writing in the series.

title: NieR: Automata
note: 88 MC; 8M+ copies; most-cited game in academic and fan critical discourse of the era; user scores consistently 9+/10 long-tail — the gap between press and player is the story.

title: Silent Hill 2 Remake
note: 86 MC; the remake no one believed Bloober could deliver, delivered; monster redesigns and fog-heavy lighting push the original's atmosphere further.

title: Resident Evil 4 Remake
note: 93 MC; essentially perfected remake; new combat feel while honoring every beat of the original.

title: Ghost of Tsushima: Director's Cut
note: 85 MC base; 10M+ sales; Iki Island DLC is tighter and more emotionally focused than the main game's third act.

title: Kingdom Come: Deliverance II
note: 88 MC; systemic medieval simulation with dramatically tightened pacing vs the first game; the most grounded RPG writing of 2025.

title: Black Myth: Wukong
note: TGA 2024 Players' Voice winner; 20M+ copies; watershed moment for Chinese AAA game development.

title: Lies of P
note: 80 MC; best non-FromSoft Soulslike to date; the Pinocchio framing is surprisingly resonant and the weapon assembly system rewards experimentation.

title: Indiana Jones and the Great Circle
Marvel's Spider-Man 2
note: 90 MC; two-hero structure and Venom arc; fastest-selling PS5 exclusive at launch.

title: Dead Space Remake
note: 89 MC; audio design alone earns its rank; honored every beat of the original while tightening the pacing and giving Isaac a coherent voice.

title: Armored Core VI: Fires of Rubicon
Like a Dragon: Infinite Wealth
note: 89 MC; Ichiban saga's boldest entry; Hawaii setting and expanded job system are a series high in both ambition and execution.

title: Monster Hunter: World
note: 90 MC; 25M+ copies; the game that brought Monster Hunter mainstream in the West and made the hunt loop feel fluid for the first time.

title: It Takes Two
note: 89 MC; TGA GOTY 2021; every chapter rebuilds its mechanics from scratch; the most celebrated co-op experience ever made and the rare GOTY winner that earns unanimous agreement.

title: Monster Hunter Wilds
note: Series peak for onboarding and seamless world design; wound mechanics and Living World biomes are genuine advances over World.

title: World of Warcraft: The War Within
Age of Empires II: Definitive Edition
Disco Elysium: The Final Cut
note: 97 OC; multiple BAFTAs; the best-written video game of the era — possibly of any era.

title: Hades
note: 93 MC; Hugo Award winner; best-in-class run-based narrative integration — every death advances the story meaningfully.

title: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33
note: 92 MC; multiple TGA 2025 wins; breakout debut that set a new gold standard for indie-scale RPG craft, art direction, and parry-based timing combat.

title: God of War Ragnarök
note: 94 MC; most TGA nominations in show history; masterclass narrative closure for the Norse arc.

title: Bloodborne
note: 92 MC; player devotion unmatched on this list — still speedrun, modded, and dissected a decade on; Gothic atmosphere and interconnected world design set a ceiling no soulslike has since cleared.

title: The Last of Us Part II Remastered
note: 93 MC base; No Return roguelike mode + PS5 polish elevate an already generational craft benchmark for character performance and animation.

title: Demon's Souls
note: 92 MC; PS5 launch showpiece; remake that respected the skeleton without sanding off the spikes.

title: Street Fighter 6
note: 92 MC; TGA 2023 Best Fighting Game; revived the franchise after SF5's troubled launch; World Tour, Modern controls, and rollback netcode make it the most accessible-yet-deep fighter ever shipped.

title: Astro Bot
note: TGA GOTY 2024; 94 MC; maxes PS5 platform craft — every level is built around a new DualSense trick, and every trick earns its moment.

title: Tekken 8
note: 90 MC; TGA 2024 Best Fighting Game; Heat System and Aggressive play style reinvigorate the 3D fighting formula; best story mode in franchise history.

title: Returnal
note: BAFTA GOTY 2022; 86 MC; DualSense authored into the combat loop — a genuine PS5-defining experience that nothing before or after has quite replicated.

title: Final Fantasy VII Rebirth
note: 92 MC; the middle act that justified the remake trilogy; open-world exploration is the most expansive the series has ever attempted.

title: Marvel's Spider-Man
note: 87 MC; defined traversal feel for a generation; best-selling superhero game, and the emotional baseline all its successors build on.

title: Horizon Forbidden West
note: 88 MC; PS5's most technically dazzling open world — machine design and biome variety remain a visual benchmark.

title: Uncharted 4: A Thief's End
note: 93 MC; Drake's farewell executed with dignity; set-pieces and character writing that defined PS4's creative peak.

title: Ratchet & Clank: Rift Apart
note: 88 MC; only game on this list whose core conceit fundamentally requires the PS5 SSD — dimensional rifting is not a cutscene trick.

title: Diablo IV
note: 88 MC; revived the ARPG kingpin with a dark tone, open world, and seasonal content; Vessel of Hatred expansion deepened the end-game loop substantially.

title: Ghost of Yōtei
note: Sucker Punch sharpens Tsushima's combat and trims open-world bloat; Atsu's arc is the stronger protagonist story.

title: Gran Turismo 7
note: 87 MC; the PS5 racing benchmark — 500+ cars, Scapes photography, GT Café career arc; DualSense trigger resistance for brake feel is the best haptic-to-racing integration on the platform.

title: Final Fantasy XVI
note: 87 MC; boldest tonal swing the mainline series has ever made; Eikon battles are spectacle benchmarks even if the open-world connective tissue sags.

title: Outer Wilds
note: BAFTA GOTY 2020; 85 MC; the most sustained "don't spoil it" reputation in gaming — the game that inspired a generation of designers to think in systemic mysteries.

title: Guilty Gear Strive
note: 87 MC; sparked the current fighting game renaissance — rollback netcode and streamlined systems brought millions of new players to the genre and proved accessible-yet-deep is achievable.

title: Doom: The Dark Ages
note: ~88 MC (May 2026 launch); shield-bash parry loop reimagines the formula; id's boldest design swing since 2016 — slower, heavier, and more deliberate than Eternal.

title: Spider-Man: Miles Morales
note: 85 MC; PS5 launch-window showcase; DualSense tuned for web traversal; Miles' emotional arc is more focused than Peter's in the first game.

title: Helldivers 2
note: 82 MC; 12M+ copies in months; the biggest PS5 cultural moment of 2024 — democratically governed galactic war, Automaton twist, and true player-driven narrative redefined live-service co-op.
